definition: A framework from Justice Jackson's concurrence in Youngstown Sheet & Tube v. Sawyer for analyzing presidential power. Zone 1: President acts with congressional authorization (maximum power). Zone 2: President acts without congressional guidance (twilight zone). Zone 3: President acts contrary to Congress's will (lowest ebb of power).
examples: The government argued the tariffs fall in Zone 1 because IEEPA authorizes them; challengers argued Zone 3 because Congress reserved tariff power to itself
